item.itunes:summary: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AIDqBMCeeGM 本频道中间不插播广告，请多多分享。低费用移民和留学，办理护照，开美国银行账户支持网银开美股，请联系Klaus本人Telegram/Whatsapp/Line/Kakao/signal+16612377888推特搜索頻道名，联系邮箱：evilccpvirus@gmail.com（下面加入聊天群）USDT打賞TRC20 TJ7XoX4dXEyJuGAVy6cXGsHGZCSVQE2WPS 投资虚拟货币，一定要看平台的排名，以下都是正规排名靠前的大平台， 欧易支持人民币购买，手续费折扣链接https://www.okx.com/join/15445906 芝麻开门支持人民币购买虚拟货币，手续费8折 https://www.gate.io/signup/9965669 币安，全球最大的https://accounts.binance.com/en/register?ref=357453871 适合海外用户，无中资背景，不支持人民币的以下平台 KuCoin https://www.kucoin.com/r/af/QBSSS4FW MEXC https://m.mexc.com/auth/signup?inviteCode=1QdTT 加入聊天交流群，以下是国语平台只可加入一个： Telegram：https://t.me/+g0g5EaCwvRI5MjNh https://t.me/+S2zAcGST12jlxhDK https://t.me/+TsKECc1XGd6o7Fhm WhatsApp: https://chat.whatsapp.com/BS4BHjGSFle6C4BSlhbThK Discord: https://discord.gg/aqq7Nhq Telegram台湾网友群https://t.me/+0Rg1eahLD1M0Y2Ix 粤语https://t.me/joinchat/TvPtlhYQ0_6hn5Z4xgdteg https://chat.whatsapp.com/GiD82dE6UBfIYRR0tATxOq 英语English https://chat.whatsapp.com/LoC4qcrlL132LDVdFhUhL6 女生群（非粉丝群，klaus不在群内）：https://t.me/joinchat/DBrFDT-51moxMDE1 完全免费还能赚钱的返利网站，先通过返利网再进入亚马逊，eBay等几千家购物网站即可获得返利 请通过我的链接注册获得$30 https://www.rakuten.com/r/FUCKCC3?eeid=37137 ==== Hi everyone, this is Klaus. I’ve predicted Xi Jinping’s third term many times before. But I still hoped that something would happen,. wishing for a miracle. Now, Xi has got his third term, which shouldn’t be a surprise. Most people saw that coming. But surprisingly, the leadership team is filled with Xi’s men. The Standing Committee was a surprise to everyone. No media outlet, Chinese or international, was accurate in their predictions. Now Xi Jinping is the boss, under whom there are:. Li Qiang, Zhao Leji, Wang Huning, Cai Qi, Ding Xuexiang and Li Xi. Cai Qi, the current secretary of the Beijing Municipal Committee,. is most famous for purging the "low-end population". Previously, he brutally removed migrant workers from Beijing. Li Qiang, the current secretary of the Shanghai Municipal Committee,. shut down the city, which led to a humanitarian disaster that attracted global attention. He specializes in destroying the middle class. The emperor Xi Jinping pursues “common prosperity”,. mainly targeting celebrities and rich people, namely, the “high-end population”. People online call the three of them the “dream iron trio”. For the Chinese, there will be NO ESCAPE from the iron fist. In fact, the other senior officials are not to be messed with either. Zhao Leji, in charge of the Central Commission for Discipline Inspection (CCDI), is Xi's enforcer. In the name of anti-corruption, he has been eliminating Xi's political enemies over the years,. strengthening Xi's dictatorship. Wang Huning is the mastermind of Xi's propaganda campaigns,. the chief engineer of Xi-ism,. “Community of Common Destiny for Mankind”, “Common Prosperity”,. and Xi’s cult of personality, putting Xi on the altar. Ding Xuexiang and Li Xi are also members of Xi’s inner circle. Foreign minister Wang Yi and the “internal circulation” expert He Lifeng have also entered the Politburo. On the other hand, Li Keqiang unceremoniously retired. Even moderate reformists like Hu Chunhua and Wang Yang were removed directly. These popular candidates for the premiership didn’t even make it to the Politburo. After the 20th National Congress, China has completely become Xi’s property. It is not an exaggeration to say that Xi getting a third term is him ascending the throne. Despite all this, Xi's ascension to the throne has not been smooth. The dismissal of former party leader Hu Jintao has drawn global attention. In the video, we can see that Hu was very reluctant to leave. This shows that there is still a lot of pressure within the party. and Xi couldn’t take the throne blatantly. In a democratic country, a successful re-election of a leader is celebrated. But Xi Jinping's re-election was very low-key. No media in China can mention the word “re-election”. Not even the phrase “third term” can be mentioned. Only the Russian Embassy congratulated Xi on the start of his third term on Weibo. After all, China is a loyal son to its Russian dad. The 20th National Congress greatly demonstrated China's so-called “full process democracy”. Raise a hand if you disagree. None. None. None. It’s unbelievable that in the 21st century. a national leader can still be elected via a show of hands. It’s amazing how Little Pinks still think China is a real democracy and the West isn’t. Xi Jinping has ascended the throne, ruling over China with his lap dogs. No matter how much Xi Jinping brags about his victory,. data of investors around the world doesn’t lie. On the first trading day after Xi's re-election was confirmed,. stock markets around the world rose,. but China and Hong Kong’s stocks plunged. The Hang Seng Index in Hong Kong fell below 1,000 points,. the biggest one-day drop since the financial crisis in 2008. Hong Kong’s stock market has fallen back to how it was in 1997, the year of the handover. “Hong Kong will remain unchanged for 50 years,” they said. They meant the stock market index. Chinese stocks plummeted across the board. JD, PDD, NIO, etc., all dropped by over 20%. Someone has analyzed the impact of “Common Prosperity” on businesses,. “Alibaba and Tencent ‘actively’ donated 100 billion RMB each. The donation, while no big deal in and of itself,. affects the foundation of the modern business structure. It opens Pandora's box: the rules of modern business have been broken. A business now has to pay tribute even though it hasn’t violated any laws. This opens Pandora’s box.”. Democracy is not merely a political issue. A democratic system manifests itself in all aspects of society. The stock market is also a product of a democratic system. A listed company is not owned by the CEO or the management team. It belongs to its millions of stockholders. Just like the government of a democratic country,. the management team is hired by shareholders to manage the company and create profits. If the managers do a poor job, the shareholders can fire them. Alibaba and Tencent donated 100 billion RMB, for which no shareholders voted. Under Xi's rule, everything is owned and can be exploited by the state apparatus. Sooner or later, China’s stock market will become a way in which Xi sucks the people dry. A brainwashed Little Pink financial blogger,. whose blind loyalty clouded his judgment,. bet on Xi Jinping before the 20th National Congress,. and lost tens of thousands of RMB when China and Hong Kong’s stocks crashed. Xi's 20th Congress had a few things worth noting. First, it highlighted the regulation of wealth accumulation. and gradual achievement of “common prosperity”. Xi previously did say that economic growth was not the goal,. and that economic development was no longer a priority. China's economy will have some dark days ahead. If economic development is not a priority, then political struggle will be. The 20th National Congress has incorporated “the spirit of struggle”. and “the strengthening of struggle skills” into the Party Constitution. During the Hu-Wen era, when China was far more open,. Wen Jiabao said something that sounds like a prophecy for today. Without a successful reform of the political system,. no economic reform will last; we might even lose our previous achievements; new societal issues that arise. will not be addressed fundamentally; historic tragedies such as the Cultural Revolution. might even happen again. Second, the fight against Taiwanese independence has been written into the Party Constitution. As Xi Jinping's power grows,. so does the probability of a war in the Taiwan Strait. Xi had been leaping backwards for quite some time. However, there were factions within the party that occasionally slammed on the brakes. After the 20th National Congress, there will be no more discord from within the party. I believe that Xi Jinping will soon begin leaping backwards faster and faster. China’s future is worrying. All right, time to hear your comments. In our last episode, we talked about the Beijing Bridge Man. John Doe said,. “I think the greater significance of the Beijing Bridge Man. is that he showed the outside world that since June 4, 1989,. the Chinese had not completely bent the knee,. and that many Chinese are eager to overthrow the CCP. In a sense, it might boost the confidence of the democratic world,. because this event has shown that. the CCP doesn’t have a solid power base.”. Netizen black gate said,. “My neighborhood was shut down the day before yesterday,. and mass COVID testing has been done every day. I do not have the courage and perseverance of Peng Lifa,. who hung banners on a bridge and blared loudspeakers. Late last night, when no one was around,. I put on my gloves, wrote Peng Lifa’s anti-CCP message,. and posted it on the bulletin board in the neighborhood. That was the only way I could honor Warrior Peng.”. Netizen Joanna Hao said,. “I spread Peng’s message in every toilet I visit. I'm going to carry a marker with me!”. Some people say that printers can be traced,. and that it’ll be better to write leaflets by hand. In addition, be sure not to leave fingerprints behind. For safety reasons, wear gloves before touching the paper. Well, that's it for this video! Like, subscribe, comment and share! This is Klaus. See you next time!

The dismissal of former party leader Hu Jintao has drawn global attention. In the video, we can see that Hu was very reluctant to leave. This shows that there is still a lot of pressure within the party.

and Xi couldn’t take the throne blatantly. In a democratic country, a successful re-election of a leader is celebrated. But Xi Jinping's re-election was very low-key. No media in China can mention the word “re-election”. Not even the phrase “third term” can be mentioned. Only the Russian Embassy congratulated Xi on the start of his third term on Weibo. After all, China is a loyal son to its Russian dad. The 20th National Congress greatly demonstrated China's so-called “full process democracy”. Raise a hand if you disagree. None. None. None. It’s unbelievable that in the 21st century. a national leader can still be elected via a show of hands. It’s amazing how Little Pinks still think China is a real democracy and the West isn’t. Xi Jinping has ascended the throne, ruling over China with his lap dogs. No matter how much Xi Jinping brags about his victory,. data of investors around the world doesn’t lie. On the first trading day after Xi's re-election was confirmed,. stock markets around the world rose,.

but China and Hong Kong’s stocks plunged. The Hang Seng Index in Hong Kong fell below 1,000 points,. the biggest one-day drop since the financial crisis in 2008. Hong Kong’s stock market has fallen back to how it was in 1997, the year of the handover. “Hong Kong will remain unchanged for 50 years,” they said. They meant the stock market index. Chinese stocks plummeted across the board. JD, PDD, NIO, etc., all dropped by over 20%. Someone has analyzed the impact of “Common Prosperity” on businesses,. “Alibaba and Tencent ‘actively’ donated 100 billion RMB each. The donation, while no big deal in and of itself,. affects the foundation of the modern business structure. It opens Pandora's box: the rules of modern business have been broken. A business now has to pay tribute even though it hasn’t violated any laws. This opens Pandora’s box.”. Democracy is not merely a political issue. A democratic system manifests itself in all aspects of society. The stock market is also a product of a democratic system. A listed company is not owned by the CEO or the management team. It belongs to its millions of stockholders.

Just like the government of a democratic country,. the management team is hired by shareholders to manage the company and create profits. If the managers do a poor job, the shareholders can fire them. Alibaba and Tencent donated 100 billion RMB, for which no shareholders voted. Under Xi's rule, everything is owned and can be exploited by the state apparatus. Sooner or later, China’s stock market will become a way in which Xi sucks the people dry. A brainwashed Little Pink financial blogger,. whose blind loyalty clouded his judgment,. bet on Xi Jinping before the 20th National Congress,. and lost tens of thousands of RMB when China and Hong Kong’s stocks crashed. Xi's 20th Congress had a few things worth noting. First, it highlighted the regulation of wealth accumulation. and gradual achievement of “common prosperity”. Xi previously did say that economic growth was not the goal,. and that economic development was no longer a priority. China's economy will have some dark days ahead. If economic development is not a priority, then political struggle will be. The 20th National Congress has incorporated “the spirit of struggle”. and “the strengthening of struggle skills” into the Party Constitution. During the Hu-Wen era, when China was far more open,.

Wen Jiabao said something that sounds like a prophecy for today. Without a successful reform of the political system,. no economic reform will last; we might even lose our previous achievements; new societal issues that arise. will not be addressed fundamentally; historic tragedies such as the Cultural Revolution. might even happen again. Second, the fight against Taiwanese independence has been written into the Party Constitution. As Xi Jinping's power grows,. so does the probability of a war in the Taiwan Strait. Xi had been leaping backwards for quite some time. However, there were factions within the party that occasionally slammed on the brakes. After the 20th National Congress, there will be no more discord from within the party. I believe that Xi Jinping will soon begin leaping backwards faster and faster. China’s future is worrying. All right, time to hear your comments. In our last episode, we talked about the Beijing Bridge Man. John Doe said,. “I think the greater significance of the Beijing Bridge Man.

is that he showed the outside world that since June 4, 1989,. the Chinese had not completely bent the knee,. and that many Chinese are eager to overthrow the CCP. In a sense, it might boost the confidence of the democratic world,. because this event has shown that. the CCP doesn’t have a solid power base.”. Netizen black gate said,. “My neighborhood was shut down the day before yesterday,. and mass COVID testing has been done every day. I do not have the courage and perseverance of Peng Lifa,. who hung banners on a bridge and blared loudspeakers. Late last night, when no one was around,. I put on my gloves, wrote Peng Lifa’s anti-CCP message,. and posted it on the bulletin board in the neighborhood. That was the only way I could honor Warrior Peng.”. Netizen Joanna Hao said,. “I spread Peng’s message in every toilet I visit. I'm going to carry a marker with me!”. Some people say that printers can be traced,. and that it’ll be better to write leaflets by hand.

In addition, be sure not to leave fingerprints behind. For safety reasons, wear gloves before touching the paper. Well, that's it for this video! Like, subscribe, comment and share! This is Klaus. See you next time!

item.itunes:summary: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dI8bwsT7kN0 本频道中间不插播广告，请多多分享。低费用移民和留学，办理护照，开美国银行账户支持网银开美股，请联系Klaus本人Telegram/Whatsapp/Line/Kakao/signal+16612377888推特搜索頻道名，联系邮箱：evilccpvirus@gmail.com（下面加入聊天群）USDT打賞TRC20 TJ7XoX4dXEyJuGAVy6cXGsHGZCSVQE2WPS 投资虚拟货币，一定要看平台的排名，以下都是正规排名靠前的大平台， 欧易支持人民币购买，手续费折扣链接https://www.okx.com/join/15445906 芝麻开门支持人民币购买虚拟货币，手续费8折 https://www.gate.io/signup/9965669 币安，全球最大的https://accounts.binance.com/en/register?ref=357453871 适合海外用户，无中资背景，不支持人民币的以下平台 KuCoin https://www.kucoin.com/r/af/QBSSS4FW MEXC https://m.mexc.com/auth/signup?inviteCode=1QdTT 加入聊天交流群，以下是国语平台只可加入一个： Telegram：https://t.me/+g0g5EaCwvRI5MjNh https://t.me/+S2zAcGST12jlxhDK https://t.me/+TsKECc1XGd6o7Fhm WhatsApp: https://chat.whatsapp.com/BS4BHjGSFle6C4BSlhbThK Discord: https://discord.gg/aqq7Nhq Telegram台湾网友群https://t.me/+0Rg1eahLD1M0Y2Ix 粤语https://t.me/joinchat/TvPtlhYQ0_6hn5Z4xgdteg https://chat.whatsapp.com/GiD82dE6UBfIYRR0tATxOq 英语English https://chat.whatsapp.com/LoC4qcrlL132LDVdFhUhL6 女生群（非粉丝群，klaus不在群内）：https://t.me/joinchat/DBrFDT-51moxMDE1 完全免费还能赚钱的返利网站，先通过返利网再进入亚马逊，eBay等几千家购物网站即可获得返利 请通过我的链接注册获得$30 https://www.rakuten.com/r/FUCKCC3?eeid=37137 ==== Hello, this is Klaus. China’s 20th National Congress is officially underway. Someone made a Weibo parody:. “Congrats to Xi Jinping on becoming the 1st emperor of the Chinese Empire”. followed by “congrats to His Majesty from Russia, North Korea, Kazakhstan, etc.”. “What is the Chinese Empire? Professor Zhang Weiwei has the answer.”. You are also advised to “buy a portrait of His Majesty Xi”. “How to properly revere the portrait of His Majesty.”. “Attention! Yellow will be banned. A guide on how to handle yellow items in your house.”. “People in many provinces took to the streets to celebrate the arrival of imperialism.”. “Democracy is a dead end. Imperialism is the only way.”. “The emperor to begin selecting new concubines on June 4.”. Ha ha, people on the Internet are really talented. If the Internet had existed when Yuan Shih-Kai ascended the throne,. it would’ve probably looked like this. As China's 20th National Congress drew near,. anxiety and fear could be felt everywhere. China’s draconian COVID measures, many believe, are purely political. Lockdowns are in effect military takeovers for the sake of the 20th National Congress. However, as the 20th National Congress was to begin, in the heart of Beijing,. a guy put up a banner on the Sithong Bridge demanding Xi Jinping to step down. “NO to nasal swabs. YES to daily bread. NO to lockdowns. YES to freedom. NO to lies. YES to dignity. NO to the Cultural Revolution. YES to reform. NO to supreme leaders. YES to ballots. NO to slavery. YES to citizenship. Go on strikes, workers and students. Fire that dictator and thief Xi Jinping.”. Several sources confirmed that the brave man was Peng Lifa, who worked in Beijing. He was soon taken away by the police, his fate still unknown. At first, many people laughed at and dismissed what he did. But his actions have ignited a flame. The Chinese are already living in misery. The collapsing economy and draconian COVID policies in the past two years have only made things worse. Thus, the impact of Peng’s actions was deafening. First of all, the Chinese who had been on their knees for too long finally saw someone stand up. Venting their long-suppressed grievances,. they spread photos and videos of the Sithongqiao incident in large numbers. The government reacted immediately, banning millions of WeChat accounts. Even the song “Sithongqiao” was banned. China even hired bridge watchers in busy areas. Despite all this, people continued to spread the message in creative ways. For example, some people commented on Dr. Li Wenliang's Weibo account,. “Yesterday, someone stood up. The Chinese are not entirely spineless.”. Some even used pictures of Peng Dehuai instead. The Sithongqiao incident also drew worldwide attention,. making headlines on major media outlets around the globe. Some overseas Chinese have also voiced their support. As I said before, I don't encourage this kind of action,. no matter how righteous it is. I don't want others to pay the price of my words. But I still greatly admire such a brave act. Peng has a martyr’s spirit not found in keyboard warriors and trolls. Peng reminds me of Tan Sitong of the Hundred Days Reform. Tan could’ve escaped, but chose to die trying to awaken the Chinese. But at that time, information technology wasn’t well developed. But things are different now, despite China's draconian online censorship. Peng's act has inspired many Chinese to fight for their lives. People are spreading his message in various ways. I think this event could become a major event in Chinese history. It could trigger the color revolution and non-violent non-cooperation movement. Many overseas Chinese supported Peng in front of Chinese Embassies,. by wearing Han costumes,. by posting Peng’s message in both Chinese and English on their school's bulletin board. Someone even wrote it at the entrance of their school. However, I don’t encourage doing this in China. We need to use our wisdom when fighting against the CCP. I think the following methods are worth promoting. Print out flyers at home, and distribute them wearing masks and sunglasses. in un-monitored areas. Put them on bulletin boards, telephone poles, public toilet doors, park benches, bus stops, etc. Public toilets are a great option for there are no cameras. People go in and out. It’s very safe and effective. Or simply stick it in places with a large flow of people. It’ll be even better to encourage people to make copies and spread them. A netizen has suggested spreading the message via Airdrop. to people in the subway, on the square, in the park, etc. Apple's Airdrop is available by default to everyone in your vicinity,. and your privacy can be well protected. Just don’t use your real name in your Apple ID settings. This tactic is worth learning and promoting. It applies to not only the story of Sithongqiao,. but also to other truths censored by the government. If you spread the word on the Internet, you might get censored easily. China’s real-name policy makes it easy for the authorities to trace you. By contrast, our offline method is safer, more effective, and difficult for the CCP to control. One online admin controls hundreds of servers, hundreds of WeChat groups, with the help of Artificial Intelligence. But to censor offline info, the CCP would require huge amounts of manpower and resources. So many people support the CCP rule only because of the CCP’s propaganda. I believe that as long as everyone takes action, the power of the people will be formidable. It will be enough to combat the CCP’s propaganda machine. In fact, Falun Gong followers have been doing this all along. I’ve no connection with them, so I will not comment on them. But they have benefitted me in the past. My awakening came when I picked up their pamphlet in elementary school,. containing lots of information on the bloody and sordid history of the CCP and its senior officials. The pamphlet planted a seed of rebellion in my young mind. Later on, when I saw criticisms towards China, I wouldn't feel uncomfortable. Slowly, I woke up. Many people underestimate the importance of spreading the truth. Have a look at the Sithongqiao incident. Imagine if a child sees this. He might think, "Oh, there are still such voices in China. It turns out that not everyone loves Xi Jinping.”. This might change his life. I encourage everyone to try and spread the truth safely. All right, time to hear your comments. In our last episode, I talked about my upbringing in one of China’s rural areas,. what China’s countryside was really like, and whether Chinese peasants were kind. Hummel Lai said,. “I just saw a little pinky tweeting about this movie a while ago, saying how touching it was,. and how the movie made him realize there are still so many poor people in China. I asked him, ‘You are feeling sad about the suffering of your compatriots deserted by the government in the movie. And yet, you insist that the Taiwanese people on the other side of the Strait must accept such a regime. What kind of confusing logic is this?’. A while later, I couldn't find my message anymore.”. Netizen A-mei said,. “Klaus, my upbringing is very similar to yours. I was born in 1991,. in a rural village in southern China. I grew up watching my fellow villagers fighting over scraps. Even brothers brutalized brothers. My life as a child was also very difficult. I lived in a dirt house. Springs in southern China were wet and cold. Our house was a bit like the sewers. The walls were leaking everywhere. Our clothes and blankets were all wet. Our family of six people were crammed into a small house,. which was like a pigsty. When my grandpa died, his body still in bed,. my mother and my uncle were fighting. Neither of them wanted the funeral to take place in their own home. The first thing my uncle did was to go to a feng shui master. to see where my grandfather should be buried. Now I’ve severed ties with all my relatives. Some people accuse me of being heartless. I don’t care. I could do without those relatives.”. Frank Fun said,. “I grew up in the rural areas of Henan, and I agree with you! In our village, one could get away with murder. It is a very depressing, lifeless village. consisting mostly of old people and little children. The elderly are very lonely (such as my grandparents). The suicide rate is really high. China’s rural areas can be summarized as follows: poor, backward, ignorant, stupid and dead. Netizen zhao xiaolong said,. “I agree with Klaus: the CCP is evil, but the Chinese people are not kind either. I grew up in a combination of urban and rural areas. The conditions were better than the countryside,. but my father was from the countryside. I would often go back to the countryside, when I was young and ignorant. Going back was like an adventure. The more I grew up, the more I realized that people of the countryside were no angels. Rural people (no offense) are cunning. They worship the rich and look down on the poor. They are mean, stingy, and even vicious to their poorer neighbors,. but flattering and subservient to people from the city or with a little power. Even if their own families are dying of hunger,. they still hold a feast for their rich distant relatives. They are idle people. spending their days gossiping about the misfortunes of others. Spreading rumors and judging others are their hobbies. I later on read Lu Xun and realized this had always been the nature of the Chinese. I'd like to tell you about something I can’t forget. A couple from the countryside rented a house near our home. They had a 10-year-old daughter not attending school,. and a son about 5 years old. The daughter would always watch while the parents and the youngest son ate. The parents never asked the daughter if she wanted to eat. Only when the son and the parents were done eating. did the daughter go over to eat by herself, when there wasn’t much left on the table. Their own daughter was treated as less than a stranger. However, the girl’s expression was devoid of suffering. At that time, I just felt that something was off. Even to this day in China’s countryside, daughters are sold so that sons could get married. Many scholars blame it all on poverty, but I disagree. You can’t simply blame the ugliness and evil of human nature on poverty.”. Well, that's it for this video. Please like, subscribe, comment and share! This is Klaus. See you next time.

Hello, this is Klaus. China’s 20th National Congress is officially underway. Someone made a Weibo parody:. “Congrats to Xi Jinping on becoming the 1st emperor of the Chinese Empire”. followed by “congrats to His Majesty from Russia, North Korea, Kazakhstan, etc.”. “What is the Chinese Empire? Professor Zhang Weiwei has the answer.”. You are also advised to “buy a portrait of His Majesty Xi”. “How to properly revere the portrait of His Majesty.”. “Attention! Yellow will be banned. A guide on how to handle yellow items in your house.”. “People in many provinces took to the streets to celebrate the arrival of imperialism.”. “Democracy is a dead end. Imperialism is the only way.”. “The emperor to begin selecting new concubines on June 4.”. Ha ha, people on the Internet are really talented. If the Internet had existed when Yuan Shih-Kai ascended the throne,. it would’ve probably looked like this. As China's 20th National Congress drew near,. anxiety and fear could be felt everywhere. China’s draconian COVID measures, many believe, are purely political. Lockdowns are in effect military takeovers for the sake of the 20th National Congress. However, as the 20th National Congress was to begin, in the heart of Beijing,. a guy put up a banner on the Sithong Bridge demanding Xi Jinping to step down. “NO to nasal swabs. YES to daily bread. NO to lockdowns. YES to freedom. NO to lies. YES to dignity.

NO to the Cultural Revolution. YES to reform. NO to supreme leaders. YES to ballots. NO to slavery. YES to citizenship. Go on strikes, workers and students. Fire that dictator and thief Xi Jinping.”. Several sources confirmed that the brave man was Peng Lifa, who worked in Beijing. He was soon taken away by the police, his fate still unknown. At first, many people laughed at and dismissed what he did. But his actions have ignited a flame. The Chinese are already living in misery. The collapsing economy and draconian COVID policies in the past two years have only made things worse. Thus, the impact of Peng’s actions was deafening. First of all, the Chinese who had been on their knees for too long finally saw someone stand up. Venting their long-suppressed grievances,. they spread photos and videos of the Sithongqiao incident in large numbers. The government reacted immediately, banning millions of WeChat accounts. Even the song “Sithongqiao” was banned. China even hired bridge watchers in busy areas. Despite all this, people continued to spread the message in creative ways. For example, some people commented on Dr. Li Wenliang's Weibo account,. “Yesterday, someone stood up. The Chinese are not entirely spineless.”. Some even used pictures of Peng Dehuai instead. The Sithongqiao incident also drew worldwide attention,. making headlines on major media outlets around the globe. Some overseas Chinese have also voiced their support. As I said before, I don't encourage this kind of action,.

no matter how righteous it is. I don't want others to pay the price of my words. But I still greatly admire such a brave act. Peng has a martyr’s spirit not found in keyboard warriors and trolls. Peng reminds me of Tan Sitong of the Hundred Days Reform. Tan could’ve escaped, but chose to die trying to awaken the Chinese. But at that time, information technology wasn’t well developed. But things are different now, despite China's draconian online censorship. Peng's act has inspired many Chinese to fight for their lives. People are spreading his message in various ways. I think this event could become a major event in Chinese history. It could trigger the color revolution and non-violent non-cooperation movement. Many overseas Chinese supported Peng in front of Chinese Embassies,. by wearing Han costumes,. by posting Peng’s message in both Chinese and English on their school's bulletin board. Someone even wrote it at the entrance of their school. However, I don’t encourage doing this in China. We need to use our wisdom when fighting against the CCP. I think the following methods are worth promoting. Print out flyers at home, and distribute them wearing masks and sunglasses. in un-monitored areas. Put them on bulletin boards, telephone poles, public toilet doors, park benches, bus stops, etc. Public toilets are a great option for there are no cameras. People go in and out. It’s very safe and effective. Or simply stick it in places with a large flow of people.

It’ll be even better to encourage people to make copies and spread them. A netizen has suggested spreading the message via Airdrop. to people in the subway, on the square, in the park, etc. Apple's Airdrop is available by default to everyone in your vicinity,. and your privacy can be well protected. Just don’t use your real name in your Apple ID settings. This tactic is worth learning and promoting. It applies to not only the story of Sithongqiao,. but also to other truths censored by the government. If you spread the word on the Internet, you might get censored easily. China’s real-name policy makes it easy for the authorities to trace you. By contrast, our offline method is safer, more effective, and difficult for the CCP to control. One online admin controls hundreds of servers, hundreds of WeChat groups, with the help of Artificial Intelligence. But to censor offline info, the CCP would require huge amounts of manpower and resources. So many people support the CCP rule only because of the CCP’s propaganda. I believe that as long as everyone takes action, the power of the people will be formidable. It will be enough to combat the CCP’s propaganda machine. In fact, Falun Gong followers have been doing this all along. I’ve no connection with them, so I will not comment on them. But they have benefitted me in the past. My awakening came when I picked up their pamphlet in elementary school,. containing lots of information on the bloody and sordid history of the CCP and its senior officials. The pamphlet planted a seed of rebellion in my young mind. Later on, when I saw criticisms towards China, I wouldn't feel uncomfortable. Slowly, I woke up.

Many people underestimate the importance of spreading the truth. Have a look at the Sithongqiao incident. Imagine if a child sees this. He might think, "Oh, there are still such voices in China. It turns out that not everyone loves Xi Jinping.”. This might change his life. I encourage everyone to try and spread the truth safely. All right, time to hear your comments. In our last episode, I talked about my upbringing in one of China’s rural areas,. what China’s countryside was really like, and whether Chinese peasants were kind. Hummel Lai said,. “I just saw a little pinky tweeting about this movie a while ago, saying how touching it was,. and how the movie made him realize there are still so many poor people in China. I asked him, ‘You are feeling sad about the suffering of your compatriots deserted by the government in the movie. And yet, you insist that the Taiwanese people on the other side of the Strait must accept such a regime. What kind of confusing logic is this?’. A while later, I couldn't find my message anymore.”. Netizen A-mei said,. “Klaus, my upbringing is very similar to yours. I was born in 1991,. in a rural village in southern China. I grew up watching my fellow villagers fighting over scraps. Even brothers brutalized brothers. My life as a child was also very difficult. I lived in a dirt house. Springs in southern China were wet and cold.

Our house was a bit like the sewers. The walls were leaking everywhere. Our clothes and blankets were all wet. Our family of six people were crammed into a small house,. which was like a pigsty. When my grandpa died, his body still in bed,. my mother and my uncle were fighting. Neither of them wanted the funeral to take place in their own home. The first thing my uncle did was to go to a feng shui master. to see where my grandfather should be buried. Now I’ve severed ties with all my relatives. Some people accuse me of being heartless. I don’t care. I could do without those relatives.”. Frank Fun said,. “I grew up in the rural areas of Henan, and I agree with you! In our village, one could get away with murder. It is a very depressing, lifeless village. consisting mostly of old people and little children. The elderly are very lonely (such as my grandparents). The suicide rate is really high. China’s rural areas can be summarized as follows: poor, backward, ignorant, stupid and dead. Netizen zhao xiaolong said,. “I agree with Klaus: the CCP is evil, but the Chinese people are not kind either. I grew up in a combination of urban and rural areas. The conditions were better than the countryside,. but my father was from the countryside.

I would often go back to the countryside, when I was young and ignorant. Going back was like an adventure. The more I grew up, the more I realized that people of the countryside were no angels. Rural people (no offense) are cunning. They worship the rich and look down on the poor. They are mean, stingy, and even vicious to their poorer neighbors,. but flattering and subservient to people from the city or with a little power. Even if their own families are dying of hunger,. they still hold a feast for their rich distant relatives. They are idle people. spending their days gossiping about the misfortunes of others. Spreading rumors and judging others are their hobbies. I later on read Lu Xun and realized this had always been the nature of the Chinese. I'd like to tell you about something I can’t forget. A couple from the countryside rented a house near our home. They had a 10-year-old daughter not attending school,. and a son about 5 years old. The daughter would always watch while the parents and the youngest son ate. The parents never asked the daughter if she wanted to eat. Only when the son and the parents were done eating. did the daughter go over to eat by herself, when there wasn’t much left on the table. Their own daughter was treated as less than a stranger. However, the girl’s expression was devoid of suffering. At that time, I just felt that something was off. Even to this day in China’s countryside, daughters are sold so that sons could get married.

Many scholars blame it all on poverty, but I disagree. You can’t simply blame the ugliness and evil of human nature on poverty.”. Well, that's it for this video. Please like, subscribe, comment and share! This is Klaus. See you next time.
